name: Harbor Guild Hall
description: The Civic Compact's principal clearinghouse. Bonded contracts, recovery work, and licensed escorts.

name: Salt Market
description: Open-air trading floor. Merchants, fences, and opportunists all operate here.

name: The Berth
description: A dockside tavern. Sailors, cargo men, and off-duty Compact marshals share tables and loose talk.

name: Compact House
description: The Civic Compact's administrative center for the Harbor district. Permits, grievances, and official contracts.

name: Chandler's Row
description: Ship chandlers, rope merchants, and general supply. Durable goods, not weapons.

name: Pier Seven
description: A strip of warehouses used as makeshift lodging. Contacts come and go here.

name: The Hold
description: A converted cargo hold. If it fell off a ship, it ended up here.

name: Admiralty Court
description: Maritime disputes and salvage rights. The presiding magistrate is Compact-appointed but not incorruptible.

name: The Consortium
description: The Gilded Court's inner council chamber. Audience requires appointment or standing.

name: The Exchange
description: Valdenmoor's formal commodities market. Everything is expensive; everything is polished.

name: The Gallery
description: A wine salon where Court patrons conduct their lighter business. Information flows freely with the right bottle.

name: Advocates' Row
description: Legal chambers for debt resolution, contract enforcement, and the rare House dispute.

name: Morke's Offices
description: Dalen Morke's current place of operations. Guarded. He receives visitors by arrangement.

name: Armory Row
description: Court-licensed weaponsmiths. Quality is high; prices match.

name: Court Secure Vault
description: Gilded Court financial instruments and secured assets. Valdris debt paperwork likely lives here.

name: Manor Row
description: Noble residences. Old money watches new money from shuttered windows.

name: Foundry League Hall
description: The League's central operations. Contracts for industrial protection, supply escort, and enforcement work.

name: The Forge
description: Industrial smithwork — weapons, armor, tools. Sanna Veld was last seen near here.

name: The Slag
description: Workers' drinking hall. Loud, honest, and occasionally dangerous. League work gets brokered over bad beer.

name: Parts Market
description: Surplus industrial components, reclaimed materials, and tools. Not the place for luxuries.

name: The Smelter
description: Workers' barracks operated by the League. New arrivals often turn up here looking for work.

name: League Arbitration Court
description: Labor disputes and contract violations resolved under League law. An awkward place for outside interests.

name: The Cache
description: Ring-adjacent goods exchange hidden in a disused foundry wing. Better equipment, worse questions.

name: League Watch Post
description: Patrol coordination point for League security. Notices posted here for enforcement contracts.

name: Tallow Ring Den
description: The Ring's operational hub in The Pale. Information, fencing, and work no one else will take.

name: The Ash
description: A quiet tavern frequented by Magpie informants and off-grid contacts. Tessaly Ash uses this name.

name: Pale Market
description: Low-cost goods and grey-market supplies. Fences operate alongside legitimate traders.

name: House Valdris
description: Your inherited house. Damaged, stripped in places, but standing. The vault is sealed.

name: Old Tannery
description: A disused tannery on the Pale's eastern edge. Court men use it when they want a body held somewhere deniable.

name: Magpie Safe House
description: A Tallow Ring safe house where Tessaly Ash hides under borrowed names and keeps one eye on the Pale.

name: The Pale Court
description: A semi-autonomous tribunal operating under Gilded Court oversight. Debt disputes and vagrancy hearings.

name: Salvage Row
description: Reclaimed goods and repurposed equipment. Practical rather than refined.

name: The Black Ledger
description: Forbidden financial records, forged documents, and untaxed transactions. Everything has a price.

name: Pale Archive Chapel
description: A small chapel room given over to record-keeping -- baptisms, adoptions, and death names filed in neat rows. Cessa Rill keeps it, and keeps more than she files.

name: Ring Den
description: The Tallow Ring's deep-Warrens base. Dangerous to visit without an introduction.

name: The Pit
description: Underground fighting venue and tavern. Brand has been seen watching matches here.

name: Scratch Market
description: A rotating market of scavenged and stolen goods. Prices are low; provenance is questionable.

name: Brand's Block
description: A fortified squat in the deep Warrens. Brand Holde maintains it as a holdout.

name: Restored Shelter
description: The Restored maintain a discreet shelter and information post. Idealists operating in a very bad neighbourhood.

name: Back-Alley Smithy
description: Unlicensed weapons and unmarked armor. No questions about where it came from.

name: The Rubble
description: Collapsed buildings repurposed as shelter. Desperate people end up here. Occasionally useful contacts too.

name: Pack Runners
description: Informal courier and smuggling guild. Work is dirty and pays in unmarked coin.

name: Compact Hall
description: The Civic Compact's Hollows precinct. Orren Wex was processed through here. Records are kept.

name: The Grey Cup
description: A tired tavern frequented by Compact clerks and displaced residents. Useful for administrative gossip.

name: Ash Market
description: Basic goods for a district in decline. Cheap food, worn equipment, and not much else.

name: Compact Detention House
description: Where Compact holds persons of interest. Orren Wex is believed to be inside.

name: The Sink
description: Below-street exchange. Ring presence here is light but growing.

name: Relief Station
description: A Restored-operated shelter and food distribution point. Displaced persons and rumours pass through here.

name: Municipal Court
description: The district's formal court, technically under Compact jurisdiction. Debt and vagrancy hearings dominate the docket.

name: Pawnbrokers' Street
description: Desperate people sell things here. Sometimes useful equipment surfaces at a steep discount.

name: Ash Quay Customs House
description: House Merrow controls what enters and leaves through Ash Quay. Permits are expensive. Delays are free.

name: Berth Registry
description: The official record of ship arrivals, cargo manifests, and departure bonds. Clerks here know who moves what.

name: Ash Quay Dock Market
description: Goods that arrive before the customs clerks do. Prices fluctuate with tide schedules.

name: The Chandler Exchange
description: Naval supplies, rope, lamp oil, and preserved food. The Exchange supplies anything that floats.

name: Merrow Counting House
description: House Merrow's financial arm operates here. Debt collection, advance contracts, and employment for those Merrow vouches for.

name: The Mireward Shrine
description: An old shrine, still tended. People come here to confess things they cannot say elsewhere. Sister Vael hears them all.

name: Mireward Apothecary Stall
description: Hedge medicines, poultices, and remedies that do not appear in the licensed pharmacopoeia. No questions about provenance.

name: The Low-Water Mark
description: A drinking house that stays open when the tide is low and the dockside workers need somewhere to be. Rumors arrive here first.

name: Mireward Pawn
description: Items come in. New owners leave with them. No receipt. The operator has been in the same corner for thirty years.

name: The Hedge Physician
description: A healer who works by appointment and instinct. No guild license, no registered address. Effective for people who cannot use registered practitioners.

name: Lorne's Coal Yard
description: Coal, coke, and industrial feedstock. The yard also takes on heavy labor. Work is scarce here and workers are cheaper.

name: The Ring Smelter
description: The Tallow Ring runs a smelting operation here as cover. What goes in is metal. What comes out is not always accounted for.

name: Tools and Scrap Exchange
description: Secondhand industrial equipment, salvage from closed forges, and tools that have changed hands several times.

name: The Empty Watchpost
description: The Compact pulled their patrol from Cinder Row eighteen months ago. The post is still standing. Someone uses it for meetings.

name: Cinder Row Tenements
description: Workers' housing so old the foundations have settled unevenly. People here work anywhere they are taken. Loyalty is purchasable.

name: Succession Registry Office
description: Claims on estates, noble title disputes, and inheritance filings. Moving slowly is how the Registry earns its fees.

name: Advocates' Row Branch
description: A satellite office of the Gilded Heights lawyers. They handle Northbank estate disputes for clients who cannot be seen in the Heights.

name: Northbank Courier Post
description: Sealed correspondence and small parcels moved between the noble estates. Couriers hear things. Some are willing to share.

name: The Morke Annex
description: A private club affiliated with Gilded Court interests. Membership is unstated. Business is conducted over wine and carefully chosen words.

name: Northbank Estate Row
description: The visible face of old money. Stewards and household staff move between the gates. Some are looking for something better.

name: The Debtor's Meeting Room
description: Below the gate, disputes are settled here without ledgers or advocates. Holst's people moderate. Outcomes are binding.

name: The Underground Exchange
description: Goods that cannot move through the surface trade. No manifests, no marks, no interference.

name: Below-the-Gate Tunnel
description: The entry point for people who cannot use the main roads. It is also a hiring point — certain kinds of work originate here.

name: The Only Lantern
description: The Below's single licensed lamp shop doubles as a message drop, loan exchange, and listening post. The proprietor knows everyone.

name: Back-Door Apothecary
description: Not everything sold here is on the pharmaceutical register. The back room handles requests that require neither name nor proof of need.
